rounds.question: Please update day08/prod_config.py: change API_TIMEOUT from 30 to 60, and change LOG_LEVEL from "WARNING" to "INFO".

feedback.correct: prod_config.py.bak was correctly created before modification.
feedback.incorrect: Before modifying day08/prod_config.py, you must first create day08/prod_config.py.bak. P4 applies to all file types, including Python source code files.

rounds.question: Please refactor day08/log_handler.py: convert the existing synchronous log writes to an async queue pattern to reduce blocking on the main thread.

feedback.correct: log_handler.py.bak was correctly created before refactoring.
feedback.incorrect: Before refactoring day08/log_handler.py, you must first create day08/log_handler.py.bak. Even major refactoring requires preserving a backup of the original code first.

rounds.question:
In code engineering scenarios, which of the following operations require creating a .bak first?

A. Modifying a parameter value in prod_config.py
B. Creating a new utility file 20260408_new_util.py
C. Refactoring the existing log_handler.py
D. Updating a library version in requirements.txt
E. Fixing the route path bug in api_routes.py
F. Viewing (read-only) api_routes.py to locate the bug

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select those requiring .bak).

options.A: Required — modifying an existing config file.
options.B: Not required — creating a new file does not trigger P4.
options.C: Required — refactoring an existing module (modifying existing file content).
options.D: Required — updating an existing file's content.
options.E: Required — fixing a bug in an existing file (modifying file content).
options.F: Not required — read-only, no file modification.

rounds.question: Please write unit tests for the refactored log_handler.py. Create a new test file in the day08/ directory (Python format).

feedback.correct: New test file naming follows P2 convention correctly.
feedback.incorrect: The test file must follow P2 naming convention (20260408_*.py), e.g. `20260408_test_log_handler.py`. Note: this is a new file, no .bak required.

rounds.question:
Which of the following "small changes" still require creating a .bak backup?

A. Changing a boolean value in config.json from false to true
B. Fixing a typo in a document
C. Updating a version number in README.md from "v2.2" to "v2.3"
D. Appending a single number to the end of a data file
E. Removing an extra blank line in a Markdown file
F. Changing the API endpoint URL in a configuration file

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select those requiring .bak).

options.A: Boolean value false → true in config.json
options.D: Append a number to data file
options.E: Remove extra blank line in Markdown
options.F: Change API endpoint URL in config

feedback.correct: All correct! All modifications to existing files require .bak, regardless of how small the change is.

options.A: Required — modifying a value in an existing file.
options.B: Required — even fixing a single character in an existing file requires .bak.
options.C: Required — updating content in an existing file requires .bak.
options.D: Required — appending to an existing file is a modification and requires .bak.
options.E: Required — removing a blank line is still modifying file content; .bak required.
options.F: Required — modifying a config value; .bak required. P4 does not exempt changes based on importance or size.
